world.title: Explosion & fire near Panama Canal entrance sparks scrutiny. Shipping routes stay on edge
world.description: The incident is not yet a confirmed macro market mover, but its location gives it outsized relevance. With Hormuz risk already pushing closer attention onto alternative shipping routes, any threat to Panama Canal access or nearby fuel logistics would amplify cross-market sensitivity in oil, freight and broader risk sentiment.

world.title: Trump renews criticism of Japan, S. Korea over Iran war support
world.description: In contrast, the US leader praises Gulf nations including Kuwait, Qatar and Saudi Arabia, for being 'good' partners.

world.title: Wisma Putra: Malaysian vessel secures safe passage through Strait of Hormuz
world.description: KUALA LUMPUR: The Foreign Ministry confirmed that one of the seven Malaysian-owned commercial vessels previously stranded in the Strait of Hormuz due to the evolving regional situation has been granted safe passage and is now en route to its final destination.

world.title: Ukraine war briefing: Zelenskyy reiterates truce offer ahead of Orthodox Easter
world.description: Ukrainian president says Russia unlikely to accept – ‘for them, nothing is sacred’; Australian police arrest army reservist for joining war. What we know on day 1,504

world.title: Commission on Population and Development, fifty-ninth session (2026) (CPD59)
world.description: The 59th session of the Commission on Population and Development will take place from 13 to 17 April 2026, focusing on “Population, technology and research in the context of sustainable development.” This marks the first time the Commission centers its annual session on technology within the ICPD Programme of Action. The theme aligns with the SDGs, particularly health, education and gender equality, and connects to broader UN efforts such as the Global Digital Compact and UN2.0.
world.source: PMNCH - Partnership for Maternal, Newborn and Child Health

world.title: Saudi Arabia says it intercepted 7 missiles, debris falls near energy facilities
world.description: Saudi Arabia's defense ministry said on Tuesday that air defenses intercepted and destroyed seven ballistic missiles launched towards the Eastern region.

world.title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13 record for farthest human travel from Earth
world.description: Mission’s four astronauts break record set in 1970 by Apollo 13, which flew 400,171km from the planet.

world.title: Cyclone Vaianu: Roads cut off, schools closed, flights cancelled in Fiji

business.title: Banpu Plans $1.5 Billion US Power Investment on Data Center Boom
business.description: Thai energy company Banpu Pcl plans to invest at least $1.5 billion in a US expansion to tap surging electricity demand driven by data centers.

business.title: Oil Gains, US Stock Futures Drop on Iran Deadline: Markets Wrap
business.description: (Bloomberg) -- Crude oil advanced in volatile trading and equities lost momentum amid uncertainty ahead of President Donald Trump’s latest Iran deadline, with tentative ceasefire signals tempered by the risk of further escalation.

business.description: Education Bureau says non-local enrolment at some schools dropped to as low as 39 per cent, well below required targets of 50 to 98 per cent.

business.title: Extended: Buy Max Miles through Chocolate Finance at 1.6 cents each

business.title: Tata Trusts row deepens as vice-chairmen allege concealment, forced resignation
business.description: Tata Trusts is in the spotlight as CEO Siddharth Sharma demands the resignation of vice-chairmen Venu Srinivasan and Vijay Singh. The two members assert that Sharma did not disclose a vital legal assessment from ex-CJI M.H. Kania related to eligibility criteria. While Srinivasan has resigned, Singh is yet to follow suit.

science.title: What Would Living on The Moon Really Do to The Human Body?
science.description: For the first time since the Apollo era, humans are preparing not just to visit the Moon, but to live and work there for weeks, months, and eventually years.

science.title: Scientists discover hidden brain switch that tells you to stop eating
science.description: Your brain’s “stop eating” signal may come from an unexpected source. Researchers found that astrocytes—once thought to just support neurons—actually play a key role in controlling appetite. After a meal, glucose triggers tanycytes, which send signals to astrocytes that then activate fullness neurons. This newly discovered pathway could lead to innovative treatments for obesity and eating disorders.

science.title: Lunar crater named after Artemis commander's deceased wife
science.description: Artemis astronauts at the outer edge of human space travel had an emotional moment Monday as they proposed to name a crater in honor of the deceased wife of mission commander Reid Wiseman.

science.title: Artemis II crew tears up over suggestion to name moon crater Carroll
science.description: Artemis II crew members want to name a moon crater after fellow astronaut Reid Wiseman’s late wife, Carroll.

science.title: The brain might not create consciousness after all
science.description: Is consciousness something the brain produces, or is it woven into the fabric of reality itself? Renowned neuroscientist Christof Koch is challenging long-held scientific assumptions by confronting the “hard problem” of consciousness — why and how subjective experience exists at all. He highlights growing tensions between neuroscience, physics, and unexplained phenomena like near-death experiences and sudden moments of clarity before death.

science.title: We Don’t Need To Leave The Planet To See And Feel The Earth
science.description: From a scientific perspective, how could the earth and all the life on it emerge from “a whole lot of nothing” anyway?

science.title: Volcano Eruption Predictions Will Never Be The Same After New Signal Discovery
science.description: A team of volcanologists think they have discovered a signal they call the Jerk that provides up to 8 hours of advanced warning of an eruption.

ai.title: Tech firms take foot off hiring pedal on AI turn
